# @vllnt/convex-experiments

> Deterministic A/B experimentation — sticky variant assignment and deduped exposure tracking, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-experiments` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`. It stores
three sandboxed tables — `experiments` (the definition: weighted `variants`, lifecycle `status`,
`salt`, unique per `(scope, key)`), `assignments` (a subject's sticky variant, persisted on first
`assign`), and `exposures` (a deduped per-subject tally). A subject's variant is a pure function of
`(salt, subjectRef)` — FNV-1a hashed onto the cumulative-weight line — so it is sticky and weight-
respecting even before persistence; `assign` records it, later calls replay it. Enrollment is gated
on `running` (`draft`/`stopped`/absent return `{ variant: null }`); stopping preserves recorded data.
`logExposure` dedups exposures into one tallied row per subject, and `results` reports distinct
subjects and total exposures per variant. Time is server-sourced. The component assigns and counts
exposure; the host owns release control, the experiment/variant meaning, the opaque `subjectRef`, and
the outcome metric (measured in the host's own tables, joined on the variant).

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
